UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile in the VesselTrack application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

creationDate.description: Date and time the user profile was created.

Vessel.title: Vessel
Vessel.description: Represents a shipping vessel being tracked.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vessel entity.

TrackingEvent.title: TrackingEvent
TrackingEvent.description: Represents a single tracking event for a vessel at a specific point in time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TrackingEvent entity.

timestamp.description: Date and time of the tracking event.

status.description: The current status of the vessel (e.g., 'In Transit', 'At Port').

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Access is restricted to the user themselves.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores vessel information.  Accessible to administrators or authorized users.

params.name: vesselId
params.description: The unique identifier of the vessel.

definition.description: Stores tracking events for each vessel.

params.name: vesselId
params.description: The unique identifier of the vessel.

params.name: trackingEventId
params.description: The unique identifier of the tracking event.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability for the VesselTrack application.  Authorization independence is achieved by avoiding `get()` calls in security rules, enabling atomic operations, and simplifying debugging. This is primarily achieved through path-based ownership and denormalization where necessary.

*   **/users/{userId}/profile:** Stores user profile information. Access is restricted to the user themselves using path-based rules, providing simple and secure access control. There is no need for denormalization here because this data is only accessible by the user.
*   **/vessels/{vesselId}:** Stores vessel information.  There are no user-specific subcollections, so the entire collection will have the same security posture.
*   **/vessels/{vesselId}/tracking_events/{trackingEventId}:** Stores tracking events for each vessel.  Uses hierarchical paths for user-owned data.

This structure ensures that listing operations (`list` requests) are secure because each collection's documents share the same security posture.  There is no need to filter data based on user role or ownership within a single collection, aligning with the principle that 'rules are not filters.'
